articles.title: Afrophobia — South Africa’s migration crisis is a governance crisis
articles.description: The images emerging from South Africa in the aftermath of the 30 June marches will reverberate across the African continent for years. Families abandoning homes they had built over decades. Businesses shuttered overnight. Parents pulling children from schools. Thousands returning to countries they had long ceased to call home.

more.title: Public procurement – ‘terrible approach’ being taken
more.description: The fastest way to empower the largest number of South Africans is to have a state that works – text books delivered on time and at a decent price – not one hamstrung by complicated processes that seek to funnel resources to favoured constituencies and create opportunities for corruption, says Ann Bernstein of the Centre for Development and Enterprise.

more.title: Mngqithi Still Eyeing PSL Talent Despite Early Setbacks?
more.description: Young Africans coach Manqoba Mngqithi is understood to remain interested in signing top Betway Premiership players, despite clubs showing little willingness to sell their key talent.

more.title: Can AI Help Solve South Africa’s Logistics Crisis?
more.description: South Africa’s logistics challenges have become one of the country’s biggest constraints on economic growth. Congested ports, rail bottlenecks, infrastructure limitations and supply chain disruptions continue to affect exporters, manufacturers, retailers and consumers alike. While long-term investment in infrastructure remains essential, businesses are increasingly asking a different question: can artificial intelligence (AI) help organisations operate [...]

more.title: Dine with the stars: Cape Town’s LUXE-starred restaurants
more.description: There are meals you enjoy, and then there are meals you remember long after the last course has been cleared. Cape Town has long cemented its reputation as one of the world’s great food cities, where acclaimed chefs, exceptional local produce and breathtaking settings come together to create unforgettable dining experiences. more.title: Sentencing of convicted paedophile Iain Wares expected back in court
more.description: The 86-year-old former Rondebosch Boys' Preparatory School teacher was found guilty in March of indecently assaulting a former pupil in 1988.

more.title: Nigeria and Ghana Urge African Union Action Over South Africa ‘Afrophobic’ Protests
more.description: Gauteng — Nigeria and Ghana have formally condemned the recent surge of Afrophobic protests targeting African nationals in South Africa, urging the African Union to intervene. In a joint diplomatic push, the two West African nations are demanding that the escalating xenophobic violence be placed on the official agenda of the upcoming African Union summit. [...]

more.title: Foot and mouth disease in South Africa: how a tracking system would control outbreaks
more.description: Foot and mouth disease is common in South Africa’s wildlife reserves. There are constant efforts to make sure it doesn’t spread to farmed animals. But since 2019 the country has seen repeated outbreaks on farms. In 2026 the country’s R80 billion (US$5 billion) beef industry faced a crisis as unchecked outbreaks spread to all provinces. [...]

more.description: Romanda Hombir has been crowned Miss World South Africa 2026, earning the opportunity to represent the country at the 75th Miss World Festival in Vietnam after winning the national title on Mandela Day, 18 July, at the Barnyard Theatre in Pretoria, Cape {town} Etc reports. According to Miss World, the 27-year-old clinical audiologist, motivational speaker and model from KaNyamazane, Mpumalanga,...
